title: pinus aristata subsp. longaeva
intro: Pinus aristata subsp. longaeva, also known as the bristlecone pine, is a slow-growing, ancient species of evergreen conifer native to the high-altitude mountains of North America. The medicinal properties of Pinus aristata subsp. longaeva are attributed to its essential oils, which contain compounds like terpenes and flavonoids, exhibiting anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of bristlecone pine include reducing stress and anxiety, improving respiratory issues, and supporting immune function, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. The herbal preparations of Pinus aristata subsp. longaeva include teas, essential oils, and resin extracts, which can be used in aromatherapy, skincare, and natural remedies.
